# W22 Probe F — Dirichlet predictor informativeness

*Auto-generated by `experiments/analyze_probe_f.py`.*

## Sample

- Records: 2666
- Scenarios: 2
- Seeds: 5
- Periods: 23
- Anticipative rate (default): 0.5

## Verdict

🔴 **Dirichlet predictor does NOT beat persistence** (Wilcoxon p = 0.9992) — simplify (drop Dirichlet or use persistence)

## L1 distance comparison (lower is better — closer to actual)

| metric | Dirichlet | Persistence | Δ (Pers − Dir) |
|---|---|---|---|
| mean L1 | 1.3982 | 1.2939 | -0.1042 |
| median L1 | 1.5122 | 1.5160 | +0.0038 |

## Jaccard similarity (higher is better — more asset overlap with actual)

| metric | Dirichlet | Persistence | Δ (Dir − Pers) |
|---|---|---|---|
| mean Jaccard | 0.2672 | 0.2810 | -0.0138 |

## Per-scenario breakdown

| scenario | n | L1 Dirichlet | L1 Persistence | L1 improvement | Fraction Dir < Pers |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ASMS_mHDM_K3_v2both | 105 | 1.2848 | 1.1252 | -0.1596 | 0.3429 |
| SMS_RDM_K0 | 105 | 1.5116 | 1.4627 | -0.0489 | 0.4476 |

## Decision per backlog spec

- 🔴 Dirichlet ≈ persistence → simplify (drop Dirichlet, just use prev AMFC weights as predictor)

## Caveat

- "Actual" t+1 weights are approximated by AMFC's chosen weights at period t+1.
- This treats AMFC's choice as the ground-truth target. True actual would require a different oracle (e.g., the OOS-optimal portfolio).
- Dirichlet prediction uses default anticipative_rate = 0.5.
